page: Amazon Web Services r6g.large is a memory-optimized server instance powered by custom 64-bit ARM architecture. It features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs utilizing the AWS ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. No GPU accelerators are included.

Performance benchmarks indicate moderate single-core capabilities but overall limited multi-core and memory throughput. The stress-ng single-core CPU test yielded an average score of 1519.48, whereas the multi-core score of 3014.98 falls into the lower performance tier. Memory bandwidth tests showed 117.04 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 36.62 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Passmark CPU and memory scores are relatively low, with a database operations score of 1309.06 and a Redis SET throughput of 2.28 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r6g.large offers a budget-friendly entry point into memory-optimized ARM64 computing, trading raw processing speed and local storage for a larger memory footprint per vCPU. This resource density makes it suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not require intensive parallel computation, such as caching layers, lightweight database operations, and basic web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services r6g.large is a memory-optimized instance powered by a 2.5 GHz AWS ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor with an arm64 architecture. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPU acceleration, and it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark results show average single-core performance with a stress-ng score of 1519.48, but lower multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics. Qualitatively, the r6g.large offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand high compute density. It is best suited for workloads such as caching layers, small-scale database operations, and general-purpose memory-optimized applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6g.large is an arm64 memory-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated Neoverse-N1 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a 1 Gbps network baseline, suited for lightweight database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6g.large is a memory-optimized arm64 instance with 2 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 1 Gbps network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A cost-efficient, memory-optimized arm64 instance featuring dedicated processors and a high memory-to-core ratio for lightweight caching and database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AWS ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor at 2.5 GHz
Features 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory
Delivers 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for memory-intensive caching and lightweight database operations
